'''Jordan Thompson''' (born 4 September 1991) is a professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|sr}} or {{rlp|lf}} for the [[York Knights]] in the [[Super League]].

He has previously played for the [[Castleford Tigers]] in the [[Super League]], and on loan from [[Castleford Tigers|Castleford]] at the [[York City Knights]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]]. Thompson has also played for  [[Hull F.C.|Hull FC]], in two separate spells and the [[Leeds Rhinos]] in the Super League. He has also previously played for [[Leigh Centurions|Leigh]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]].<ref name="Statistics at thecastlefordtigers.co.uk">{{cite web|url=http://www.thecastlefordtigers.co.uk/playerprofile.php?pid=957|title=Statistics at thecastlefordtigers.co.uk|publisher=thecastlefordtigers|date=31 December 2013|accessdate=1 January 2014|url-status=dead|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20120518154332/http://www.thecastlefordtigers.co.uk/playerprofile.php?pid=957|archivedate=18 May 2012 }}</ref> Earlier in his career he played as a {{rlp|ce|fb}} and on the {{rlp|wg}}.<ref name="Jordan Thompson Profile">{{cite web|url=http://www.rleague.com/db/player/t/thompson_jordan/index.php|title=Jordan Thompson Profile|publisher=rleague.com|date=1 January 2010|accessdate=1 January 2010|url-status=dead|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20100209061857/http://www.rleague.com//db/player/t/thompson_jordan/index.php|archivedate=9 February 2010|df=dmy-all}}</ref>

==Background==
Thompson was born in [[Normanton, West Yorkshire]], [[England]].

==Early career==
He signed for the club form local amateur side [[Normanton (Rugby League)|Normanton Knights]].

==First team appearances==
===Castleford Tigers===
Thompson made his first-team début in a 50–10 loss to [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]] in round 16 of 2009's [[Super League XIV]], where he came on off the bench.<ref name="St Helens 50-10 Castleford">{{cite web|url=http://www.rleague.com/db/content/91000/91683.php |title=St Helens 50–10 Castleford |publisher=rleague.com |date=12 June 2009 |accessdate=1 January 2010 |url-status=dead |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20100206020209/http://rleague.com/db/content/91000/91683.php |archivedate= 6 February 2010 }}</ref> Since this, he made his maiden start for the club in a 40–38 win at [[Bradford Bulls|Bradford]] where he scored a try playing on the wing.<ref name="Bradford 38-40 Castleford">{{cite web|url=http://www.rleague.com/db/content/91000/91822.php |title=Bradford 38–40 Castleford |publisher=rleague.com |date=5 July 2009 |accessdate=1 January 2010 |url-status=dead |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20100314045034/http://www.rleague.com/db/content/91000/91822.php |archivedate=14 March 2010 }}</ref>

Jordan has made a steady start to life in the Castleford first team and has filled in at full back, wing and centre and acquitted himself well. Jordan signed a new contract until the end of the 2013 season in July 2011.<ref>[http://www.superleague.co.uk/article.php?id=22815 7 July 2011.]</ref>

===Hull FC===
Jordan signed a 3-year contract with [[Super League]] club [[Hull F.C.]] in September 2013 and has been a regular in the squad all through 2014 & 2015.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/24021275|title=Hull FC sign Jordan Thompson from Castleford Tigers|date=9 September 2013|publisher=|via=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>

===Leigh Centurions===
In October 2017 he joined Leigh on a 2-year deal.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/41528423 |title=Jordan Thompson: Leigh Centurions sign Hull FC forward|publisher=BBC Sport|date=6 October 2017|accessdate=24 January 2018}}</ref>

===York RLFC===
On 18 October 2021, it was reported that he had signed for [[York RLFC Knights|York RLFC]] in the [[RFL Championship]]<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=18 Oct 2021|accessdate=26 Dec 2021|title="The facilities are second to none" – York’s latest signing|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/the-facilities-are-second-to-none-yorks-latest-signing-jordan-thompson/}}</ref>
